meta.title: Centrail — Reference pitch diagnosis and correction
header.subtitle:
Reference pitch diagnosis
and correction
drop.title: Drop an audio file here, or click to choose one
drop.formats: WAV · FLAC · MP3 · OGG · M4A — your file never leaves your computer
progress.privacyNote: Processing on your device — your audio hasn't left it.
verdict.inTune.lowConf: Looks in tune, but this measurement isn't reliable (see below).
verdict.slight.lowConf: Looks slightly {dir} ({cents}), but this measurement isn't reliable.
verdict.clear.lowConf: Looks clearly {dir} ({cents}), but this measurement isn't reliable.
honesty.lowConf: Low tonal consistency ({r}%): this measurement isn't reliable.
honesty.alreadyTuned: Already in tune. Correcting it can only degrade it.
card.hist.title: Deviation histogram (cents relative to the A440 grid)
card.measurement.title: Measurement
card.file.title: File
card.file.name: Name
card.drift.title: Drift over the course of the track
card.drift.low: Max drift: {n} ¢ — tuning is stable throughout the track. A global correction is enough.
card.drift.mid: Max drift: {n} ¢ — there's mild variation during the track (typical of tape recordings). A global correction centers the average on the target, but some passages will stay slightly off.
card.drift.high: Max drift: {n} ¢ — the tuning shifts audibly during the track. This would need section-by-section correction (out of scope for this version).
correct.title: Correct to
howItMeasures.p1: The detector scans the track taking audio windows spread across its full duration. Each window gets an FFT with a Hann window, and each spectral peak's frequency is refined with parabolic interpolation, reaching resolution well below a single bin's width.
howItMeasures.p2: Each peak is compared against the nearest note on the A = 440 Hz tempered scale, and its deviation in cents (modulo 100, with circular math) feeds an energy-weighted histogram. The histogram's peak, refined with a circular mean, gives the global offset; the reference pitch is 440 × 2^(offset/1200). This method is key-independent and doesn't require separating instruments.
howItMeasures.p3: Tonal consistency shows how concentrated the histogram is: high values mean stable harmonic material; low values can mean heavy percussion, noise, or unstable tuning — not necessarily an ambiguous measurement. The drift panel splits the track into segments and estimates each one's reference pitch.
howItMeasures.p4: Correction uses Rubber Band (Finer mode, highest quality) to change pitch without touching tempo. Measuring and correcting always run on the same decode of the file — never measured one way and corrected another.
error.unsupportedFormat: Format not supported in this version ({format}). Available formats: WAV, FLAC, MP3, OGG, M4A.
